payload.title: MTA Open Data Plan 2025 Annual Update

evidence_refs.source_quote: METROPOLITAN TRANSPORTATION AUTHORITY OPEN DATA PLAN 2025 ANNUAL UPDATE
The Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA) and Open Data team
New York State (NYS) Legislature enacted the MTA Open Data Law
Governor Hochul

payload.description: Created in 2024 to facilitate internal decision-making on open data releases

evidence_refs.source_quote: In 2024, we created the Open Data Steering Committee, marking a major shift in how we facilitate internal decision-making on open data releases
updating the MTA Board on our progress and achievements
traveling to Washington, DC to present at the Transportation Research Board
CUNY Macaulay Honors College Datathon

payload.description: Presents the third annual update to the Open Data Plan, reinforcing commitment to transparency, accountability, civic engagement, and data-informed decision-making

evidence_refs.source_quote: The Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA) and Open Data team is proud to present the third annual update to the Open Data Plan, reinforcing our commitment to transparency, accountability, civic engagement, and data-informed decision-making

payload.description: Enacted by Governor Hochul and the New York State Legislature. 2024 marked three years since enactment.

evidence_refs.source_quote: 2024 marked three years since Governor Hochul and the New York State (NYS) Legislature enacted the MTA Open Data Law

payload.description: MTA's first Open Data Challenge launched in fall 2024. Stakeholders from across the country were invited to build a product using MTA open data. Over 100 submissions received.

evidence_refs.source_quote: In fall 2024, we launched the MTA's first Open Data Challenge and called on all interested stakeholders from across the country to build a product, such as a web app, data visualization, written report, map, or even a piece of art, using the MTA's open data collection

payload.description: Challenged students to solve real policy challenges facing NYC using MTA open datasets

evidence_refs.source_quote: we participated in the CUNY Macaulay Honors College Datathon, which challenged students to solve real policy challenges facing NYC using MTA open datasets

payload.description: Traveled to Washington, DC to present at the Transportation Research Board

evidence_refs.source_quote: traveling to Washington, DC to present at the Transportation Research Board

payload.description: Series of public-facing events with live demonstrations showing users how to use the operating budget datasets and explaining how they were built

evidence_refs.source_quote: a series of public-facing events we dubbed our 'budget press tour'. During these events, we held live demonstrations showing users how to use these datasets and explained how we built them

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote is not present in the cited block

payload.description: Created the Open Data Steering Committee to facilitate internal decision-making on open data releases

evidence_refs.source_quote: In 2024, we created the Open Data Steering Committee, marking a major shift in how we facilitate internal decision-making on open data releases

payload.description: Published MTA's operating budget in open data format, making MTA the first transit agency in the United States to achieve this

evidence_refs.source_quote: In the first quarter of 2024, we published MTA's operating budget in open data format, making us the first transit agency in the United States to achieve this

payload.event_name: GTFS real-time Service Alerts dataset release
payload.description: Published an archive of GTFS real-time data with the Service Alerts dataset, providing a historical record of service disruptions, planned work, and other incidents

evidence_refs.source_quote: Shortly after the release of the operating budget open datasets, we published an archive of GTFS real-time data with the Service Alerts dataset

payload.description: Series of public-facing events with live demonstrations showing users how to use the operating budget datasets and explaining how they were built

evidence_refs.source_quote: This data release was accompanied by public engagement efforts, including a series of public-facing events we dubbed our "budget press tour"
28 NEW DATASETS
144 SUPPORTED OPEN DATASETS

payload.metric_name: records in largest dataset (Bus Hourly Ridership)
payload.raw_value_text: 115,979,419 RECORDS IN OUR LARGEST DATASET, BUS HOURLY RIDERSHIP

evidence_refs.source_quote: 115,979,419 RECORDS IN OUR LARGEST DATASET, BUS HOURLY RIDERSHIP

payload.metric_name: downloads of most popular dataset (Daily Ridership)
payload.raw_value_text: 116,097 DOWNLOADS OF OUR MOST POPULAR DATASET, DAILY RIDERSHIP

evidence_refs.source_quote: 116,097 DOWNLOADS OF OUR MOST POPULAR DATASET, DAILY RIDERSHIP
20 SPEAKING ENGAGEMENTS
1050 ATTENDEES AT OPEN DATA EVENTS

payload.description: Received over 100 submissions from students, researchers, policymakers, developers, and everyday customers

evidence_refs.source_quote: We received over 100 submissions

evidence_refs.source_quote: over 160 open datasets

payload.description: Over 160 open datasets and several supporting resources

evidence_refs.source_quote: over 160 open datasets

payload.claim_text: In the first quarter of 2024, MTA published its operating budget in open data format, becoming the first transit agency in the United States to achieve this.
payload.statement: First US transit agency to publish operating budget in open data format
payload.description: Operating budget published in open data format in Q1 2024

evidence_refs.source_quote: In the first quarter of 2024, we published MTA's operating budget in open data format, making us the first transit agency in the United States to achieve this

payload.claim_text: All data visualizations on MTA's performance metrics dashboard (metrics.mta.info) are built using datasets available on the Open Data Portal, and the website is built with open-source tools.
payload.statement: metrics.mta.info is fully replicable using open data and open-source tools
payload.description: Performance metrics dashboard built entirely from Open Data Portal datasets and open-source tools

evidence_refs.source_quote: all data visualizations on MTA's performance metrics dashboard, metrics.mta.info, are built using datasets available on the Open Data Portal. The website is also built with open-source tools

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote is not present in the cited block

payload.claim_text: In 2025, the MTA Open Data team will focus efforts to publish more geospatial data to the Open Data Portal.
payload.statement: 2025 focus area: publish more geospatial data
payload.description: Basic spatial data about train stations, bus stops, and routes is inconsistent across operating agencies

evidence_refs.source_quote: In 2025, our team will focus efforts to publish more geospatial data to the Open Data Portal

payload.claim_text: In 2025, the MTA Open Data Program aims to grow the depth of data offerings beyond just the number of datasets published.
payload.description: Measuring program by dataset count alone is flawed as it does not capture depth of data available

evidence_refs.source_quote: This year, we're also aiming to grow the depth of our data offerings

payload.caption: Three datasets making up the MTA operating budget released in open data format in Q1 2024

evidence_refs.source_quote: The operating budget is available in three datasets: Statement of Operations, Subsidies, and Headcount
In the first quarter of 2024, we published MTA's operating budget in open data format

payload.caption: Datasets published to support the launch of the Congestion Relief Zone
rows: Bus routes in the Central Business District (CBD)
Taxi zones in the Central Business District (CBD)
Taxi and for-hire vehicle trips in the CBD

evidence_refs.source_quote: We published a variety of supporting datasets to help analyze congestion relief's impacts, such as bus routes and taxi zones in the Central Business District (CBD); the CBD Geofence, which is a geographic dataset of the tolling boundary that is being used by companies like Google Maps and Waze; and taxi and for-hire vehicle trips in the CBD
